rationale: The execution chain starts with winrshost.exe spawning cmd.exe, which then launches two PowerShell processes (EV-001, EV-002, A0). The PowerShell processes use encoded commands (DER-001) to create temporary PowerShell scripts (EV-003, EV-006) and then compile a .NET assembly (40sjawwp.dll) using csc.exe (EV-010, EV-011, EV-012, EV-013). This behavior, especially the compilation of a DLL from a PowerShell script, is highly indicative of malicious activity, likely for code execution or privilege escalation.
